steps.title: Find the radius
steps.content:
The diameter is $8$ cm, so the radius is
$$r=\frac{8}{2}=4$$

steps.title: Use the cone volume formula
steps.content: Substitute $r=4$ and $h=21$ into the cone volume formula: $$V=\frac{1}{3}\pi r^2h$$ $$V=\frac{1}{3}\pi(4)^2(21)=112\pi$$ Since $V=n\pi$, $n=112$. The answer is 112.
